channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Green shoots after rain give SA farmers hope after years of drought
item.description: Farmers in the Riverland and Mallee are cautiously optimistic as new growth emerges from the paddock following "meaningful rain".
item.dc:creator: Eliza Berlage and Anita Ward

media:group.media:description: Green shoots give farmers hope after years of drought in South Australia's Mallee and Riverland regions.

item.title: Two dead after car hits tree in Dandenong in Melbourne's south-east
item.description: Police say a car was travelling along the Princes Highway when the driver lost control and crashed into a tree about 1:20am.

media:group.media:description: Two people are dead after a car hit a tree on the Princes Highway in Dandenong.

item.title: Tasmania's footy turf war is over, but the Devils must now deliver
item.description: They may not be scheduled to play their first AFL game until 2028, but the Tasmania Devils have claimed their first scalp — booting Hawthorn out of the state which has been its second home for more than two decades.
item.dc:creator: Chris Rowbottom

media:group.media:description: Tasmania Devils players at first VFL game against Coburg at North Hobart oval, March 21, 2026.

item.title: Complaint from ex-boss led to investigation into embattled mental health clinic
item.description: Tasmania's largest private mental health hospital, which was given a $2 million lifeline by the state government last year, had its corporate governance placed under review, following a formal complaint by the facility's former chief executive.
item.dc:creator: Paddy Manning

media:group.media:description: An investigation into the Hobart Clinic's corporate governance was held following complaints from a former chief executive.

item.title: Warnings issued after surge in Queensland roadside close calls
item.description: Flashing lights on the roadside should mean one thing, but the RACQ says many drivers are not following their safety obligations.
item.dc:creator: Tobi Loftus

media:group.media:description: A car hits an RACQ van.

item.title: Why is there no vaccine for this Ebola outbreak?
item.description: The latest Ebola outbreak is caused by a strain of virus discovered nearly two decades ago, yet there is no vaccine. So what does this mean for people affected? And can vaccines be fast-tracked?
item.dc:creator: Jacinta Bowler

media:group.media:description: In past Ebola outbreaks, health authorities used temperature checks to try to limit the spread.

item.title: The men offering an alternative to ‘manosphere’ influencers
item.description: "Manosphere" rhetoric is getting louder online, but can men showcasing different lifestyles and values online cut through that noise?
item.dc:creator: Anna Chisholm

media:group.media:description: Caleb Ferdinand says he wants to be someone other people can relate to.

item.title: Developers targeting affluent areas under affordable housing scheme
item.description: Property developers are using an affordable housing scheme to bypass normal planning requirements and win fast-tracked approval for contentious projects across Melbourne.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Thompson

media:group.media:description: A luxury development in Hawthorn is among several high-end projects granted fast-tracked approval under the scheme.

item.title: Viral memes trying 'to catch fire on the internet' in AI Albo campaign
item.description: One start-up founder who helped spread viral memes about capital gains tax reforms says the campaign was aimed at attracting attention after the budget, and not necessarily focused on accuracy.
item.dc:creator: Adelaide Miller

media:group.media:description: Frank Greeff shared this fake photo with Prime Minster Anthony Albanese to his Instagram account last week.

item.title: Vanuatu accuses Australia and China of 'undermining' it
item.description: Jotham Napat has confirmed Vanuatu has agreed to two pacts with both countries, but is not happy with either party.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Dziedzic

media:group.media:description: Jotham Napat said he was ready to sign the Nakamal Agreement with Anthony Albanese.

item.title: Life inside one of the world's most dangerous waterways
item.description: Before rising conflict pushed the Strait of Hormuz into the spotlight as a choke point vital to economies globally, tourism transformed women's lives.
item.dc:creator: Libby Hogan

media:group.media:description: Women of the Strait of Hormuz say they have been central to preserving traditional skills and culture on the islands.

item.title: Labor walks tightrope as tax overhaul alarms startups and investors
item.description: Labor MPs are gearing up for a hard sell on the impact of the federal government's tax changes on some small businesses, trusts and shares, despite many reporting "positive" feedback on housing reforms.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: The prime minister says the government is consulting on how to ensure tax changes do not disproportionately hurt start-ups and venture capital.

item.title: Quiz: Got the Wednesday blues? Let's take a trip around the world
item.description: Can you name the city in this picture? And what is the Canadian specialty food "beaver tail"?

media:group.media:description: Copacabana beach in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil.

item.title: 'Absolute madness': How a costume rock duo are breaking the internet
item.description: French Canadian duo Angine de Poitrine are riding a wave of interest after a video of their live performance exploded in popularity, aided by a comical reaction by many on YouTube struggling to understand how their music is made.
item.dc:creator: James Dunlevie

media:group.media:description: Khn (left) and Klek have said the costumes started out as a "joke".

item.title: Putin lands in China for meeting with 'dear friend' Xi
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin smiles as he greets Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi at the airport in Beijing.

media:group.media:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin greets Chinese officials, including Foreign Minister Wang Yi after he landed in Beijing.

item.title: Far-right Israeli minister accuses ICC of making 'secret' request to arrest him
item.description: Reports have been circulating that the ICC is preparing to issue warrants against Israel's far-right finance minister, Bezalel Smotrich.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Doran

media:group.media:description: Bezalel Smotrich says ICC prosecutors are requesting to issue an arrest warrant against him.

item.title: Son arrested in Spain over billionaire fashion tycoon father's deadly 100m cliff fall
item.description: A family spokesperson confirmed Jonathan Andic was being questioned by investigators. His father, Isak Andic, died in December 2024.

media:group.media:description: Jonathan Andic arrives at court escorted by police, where he is expected to be questioned.

item.title: Australia is wasting $1 billion on car parks nobody uses, report finds
item.description: Australia is wasting more than $1 billion each year building off-street car parks that end up unused and unwanted, a Grattan report has found.
item.dc:creator: Kenji Sato

media:group.media:description: The report found the number of car parks far exceeded the number of cars in Australia's capital cities.

item.title: Two Italian divers' bodies recovered from underwater cave
item.description: Finnish divers recovered the bodies after they were located deep inside an underwater cave in the Maldives.

media:group.media:description: The plan is to recover the remaining two bodies on Wednesday.

item.title: Inquest hears elderly man who died was given another person's medication
item.description: An 82-year-old South Australian man died after he ingested prescription medication that was meant for another person and had been incorrectly labelled, a coronial inquest is told.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Carbone

media:group.media:description: The incorrectly labelled medication was collected from Chemist Warehouse pharmacy at the Parabanks Shopping Centre.

item.title: Former governor-general Peter Hollingworth dies aged 91
item.description: Peter Hollingworth was the first and only cleric to serve as governor-general, before his resignation in 2003 amid his handling of historical church abuse scandals.
item.dc:creator: Ciara Jones

media:group.media:description: Former governor-general Peter Hollingworth has died.

item.title: Father in critical condition as police probe whether altercation with hoons led to assault
item.description: Geraldton police investigate whether complaints about hooning were behind an alleged assault that left a father-of-three in a critical condition.
item.dc:creator: Piper Duffy

media:group.media:description: Father-of-three Brodie Stokes (right) is in a critical condition after an assault in a Geraldton suburb.

item.title: Undersea volcanic eruption visible from space
item.description: The Titan Ridge Volcano is erupting in the Bismarck Sea, sending ash and smoke into the sky and prompting authorities to warn of possible tsunamis in the area.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Martin and Dana Kirimof

media:group.media:description: Experts were watching the eruption closely despite its remote location.

item.title: Aussie great-grandmother to compete at Comm Games
item.description: At 75 years old, Jacky Hudson will tread an unlikely path for a great-grandmother after earning selection in Australia's Commonwealth Games lawn bowls squad.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Stewart

media:group.media:description: Jacky Hudson, 75, is headed to Glasgow with the Jackaroos.

item.title: Man charged after alleged shotgun incident in Hobart suburbs
item.description: A 27-year-old man has been charged with a raft of offences following an alleged shooting incident in Hobart's southern suburbs.

media:group.media:description: The Westpac rescue helicopter was used in the search for the man.
